title: Algebra of Complex Numbers
summary: This page introduces the graphical representation of complex numbers in the complex plane using Cartesian (rectangular) form z = a + jb, where a is the real part and b is the imaginary part. It then transitions to polar coordinate representation, expressing a complex number in terms of r and θ. The page concludes by introducing Euler's formula, e^(jθ) = cos θ + j sin θ, which connects the polar and rectangular forms.

figures.caption: The complex plane showing a complex number z = a + jb represented by point coordinates (a, b), its magnitude r and angle θ, along with its complex conjugate z* = a − jb reflected across the real axis.
